boundary.short: Boundary-flux lens. Not a hydrodynamic solver.
boundary.long: This lab renders constrained boundary loading, compression, focused release, and registered aftermath as a visual grammar. It does not solve fluid dynamics, derive sonoluminescence, or claim physical-force simulation.

kernel_claim.primary: A boundary-flux event is modeled as constrained loading followed by compression, focus formation, release, and registered trace.
kernel_claim.public_limit: The renderer is explanatory and exploratory. Physical interpretation must remain tied to the source evidence project.

phases.label: Drive loads
phases.meaning: External drive loads the boundary system.

phases.label: Boundary compresses
phases.meaning: The constrained boundary stores structured pressure.

phases.label: Focus forms
phases.meaning: Interior structure gathers into a focused release condition.

phases.label: Release emits
phases.meaning: A release event crosses the boundary.

phases.label: Trace remains
phases.meaning: The event leaves a registered aftermath.

channels.label: Interior
channels.meaning: Loaded internal state inside the boundary.

channels.label: Flux
channels.meaning: Directed transport across or within the boundary.

channels.label: Trace
channels.meaning: Registered aftermath left by the event.

presets.label: Stable Window
presets.kernel_meaning: Balanced boundary loading with readable carrier volume and restrained trace.

presets.label: Deep Collapse
presets.kernel_meaning: High boundary and interior compression without assuming clean release.

presets.label: Clean Release
presets.kernel_meaning: Focused crossing through the register with coherent aftermath trace.

presets.label: Degraded Boundary
presets.kernel_meaning: Overloaded boundary with noisy relationship walls and persistent disturbed trace.

presets.label: Aftertrace
presets.kernel_meaning: The release body fades while registered aftermath remains on the faces.

register_body.architecture_note: The renderer is shape-agnostic. Cube Register is the first face-projection body, not the permanent ontology.

sheet_clock.relationship: tick advances by elapsed_seconds * sheets_per_second; one frame step is one sheet; cycle_rate is sheets_per_second divided by sheet_count.
sheet_clock.boundary: This imports the P900-style sheet clock only. It does not import or mutate the P900 theorem artifact.

cycle_progression.rule: Sheet index wraps modulo sheet_count, but visual register phase advances continuously across completed cycles.
cycle_progression.meaning: A completed sheet pass is not a reset. It advances the continuous sheet phase without adding discontinuous cycle-memory effects.

regime_calibration.boundary: Visual calibration only. These settings expose finite boundary-flux grammar; they are not physical parameters.
regime_calibration.principle: Each regime emphasizes a different Cube Register role: stable balance, compression, focused release, overload, or aftermath trace.

k900_action_layer.boundary: K900/P19/P20 action sources are indexed, mapped, and numerically bound, but no numeric action rendering is active yet.

action_grammar.confinement: internal difference under neutral closure produces confinement
action_grammar.transformation: admissible directed channel crossing produces transformation

rendering_policy.allowed_next_step: build numeric binding packet from selected P19/P20 artifacts

inspector.boundary: Numeric binding is displayed for custody but not rendered into Cube Register geometry.

stage_grab_bar.purpose: Touch-safe page scrolling over the canvas stage.
stage_grab_bar.boundary: UI affordance only; does not alter Cube Register geometry.

autostart_animation.purpose: Kernel boot draws once and starts one requestAnimationFrame loop after data is loaded.

stage_lock.rule: Unlocked stage captures drag/pinch/wheel. Locked stage releases canvas touch for easier page scrolling.
stage_lock.boundary: UI behavior only; does not alter Cube Register geometry.

p900_style_adoption.scope: font weights, uppercase micro-labels, card/control density
p900_style_adoption.boundary: UI styling only; does not alter Cube Register geometry or K900 binding.

p900_shell_adoption.scope: topbar, instructions bar, transport strip, stage card, settings density
p900_shell_adoption.boundary: UI shell styling only; no renderer geometry or K900 numeric binding changes.

winamp_repair.boundary: UI information architecture and styling only.

winamp_information_architecture.boundary: HTML information architecture only; renderer and data bindings unchanged.

winamp_order_repair.rule: Stage, transport, mixer, then regime/leftover, audit panels, phase cards, explanation.
winamp_order_repair.boundary: CSS ordering and card readability only.

wireframe.principle: Every visible behavior must trace to a Thalean object, projection rule, finite source, or explicit exploratory parameter.

perfect_return_cell_triad.principle: The visible unit is a triad of diads coupled into the closed W-X-Y-Z-T-I-W return field.
perfect_return_cell_triad.boundary: Observer-only autonomous cycle; no user-attempted coupling.

g60_source_object.rule: G60 is the thing; visible lab forms are projections.

g60_source_binding.rule: G60 is the thing; Perfect Return Cell is a projection witness.

perfect_return_cell_source_first.rule: Source Object precedes Projection Contract in the UI.

g900_signature_binding.rule: G900 is local theorem signature only; no census claim.

perfect_return_hidden_panels.rule: Primary flow stops after Cycle Clock; lower audit panels remain source-visible but display-hidden.

cocycle_companion_receipt.rule: Display as compact companion status only; do not claim closed source derivation.

projection_modes.rule: Projection modes are declared and validated before becoming selectable UI modes.

projection_mode_selector.rule: Selector applies validated mode path in browser memory; source JSON remains default Mode 1.

edge_pill_ui.reason: Redundant and stale for Mode 2 return-path display.
edge_pill_ui.rule: Do not use edge-pill activation as a validation surface.

upstairs_downstairs_interpretation.rule: Use as explanatory role grammar for projection modes, not as proof or source-object replacement.
